title: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc (SPCE) Q2 2024 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Financial Challenges and Strategic Advancements
text: Virgin Galactic reported Q2 2024 results showing $4 million in revenue primarily from the Galactic 07 commercial space flight and astronaut membership fees. The company charged approximately $900,000 per seat for this flight. Operating expenses declined to $106 million from $141 million year-over-year. However, capital expenditures increased to $34 million compared to $10 million previously. The firm reported negative free cash flow of $114 million, an improvement from negative $135 million in the prior year period. The company maintains a strong cash position of $821 million in cash, equivalents, and marketable securities. Management projects Q3 2024 free cash flow between negative $115 million and $125 million. Key strategic developments include progress on Delta Class spaceship development, with design phases nearing completion and transition toward building and testing phases. Leadership targets commercial Delta operations by 2026 and plans serious discussions regarding a second spaceport location in 2025. The addressable market consists of approximately 300,000 individuals with net worth exceeding $10 million who prioritize experiential travel and express interest in space experiences. The company maintains a mostly stable backlog of future astronauts and targets allocating 10 percent of flights for research purposes.
